seoTitle: How to Do Anomaly Detection in Power BI Visuals
metaDescription: Use Power BI's built-in AI anomaly detection to spot unexpected patterns in your data, from fraud signals to quality control issues.
intro: Spotting irregularities in your data manually is slow and easy to miss, but Power BI's built-in AI anomaly detection can flag unexpected patterns automatically. This guide covers how to turn on and interpret anomaly detection within your visuals. It's a valuable feature for anyone doing fraud checks, quality control, or general performance monitoring.

steps.heading: Access the AI features in Power BI
steps.body: Locate the built-in AI options within your visual's settings, where anomaly detection can be enabled.

steps.heading: Set up anomaly detection visuals
steps.body: Apply anomaly detection to a time-based visual, such as a line chart, so Power BI can flag data points that deviate from expected patterns.

steps.heading: Interpret detection results
steps.body: Review the flagged anomalies to understand why they were marked as unusual and whether they represent a real issue worth investigating.

steps.heading: Apply detection to business use cases
steps.body: Use anomaly detection in scenarios like fraud checks in financial data or quality control in manufacturing metrics.

steps.heading: Integrate with other Power BI features
steps.body: Combine anomaly detection with alerts or other visuals so unusual patterns get surfaced as part of your broader reporting workflow.

faq.question: Does anomaly detection in Power BI require coding or machine learning skills?
faq.answer: No, it's a built-in AI feature that can be turned on through the visual's settings without needing coding or modeling expertise.

faq.question: What kind of visuals support anomaly detection?
faq.answer: It's most commonly used with time-based visuals like line charts, where it can identify points that deviate from the expected trend.

faq.question: Can anomaly detection help with fraud detection?
faq.answer: Yes, one common use case is flagging unusual financial transactions or figures that could indicate fraud.

faq.question: Will anomaly detection flag every unusual data point?
faq.answer: It flags points that statistically deviate from the expected pattern, though not every flagged point necessarily represents a real problem.

faq.question: Is anomaly detection available in the free version of Power BI?
faq.answer: Built-in AI visual features like anomaly detection are generally accessible in Power BI Desktop, though broader use may depend on your license.
